svg.text: Stage transitions · in-state actions · worktree fork/join (Fleet mode)
Creates isolated worktrees — each agent gets its own branch and directory.
Each worktree state is fully independent
until merged. Guards check all submitted.
Each worktree tracks its own agent status independently. Guards on transitions read these.
Dashboard action buttons are driven by agent status:
idle → Open · implementing (session running) → Attach · waiting → Focus (high priority)
all submitted + Fleet → Evaluate (high priority) · all submitted + Drive → Close / Review
error state: agent session crashes — use feature-do to restart. Dashboard shows Open for error/idle.
Fleet Research (e.g. research-setup 11 cc gg cx)
Each agent writes to their own findings file in docs/specs/research-topics/logs/.
research-submit marks one agent done (status: submitted).
When all agents submitted → research-eval reads all findings,
presents consolidated features to user, updates main research doc.
No worktrees for research — all agents work in the main repo,
each writing only to their own findings file.
The state machine is the source of truth for the dashboard UI only.
It drives: action buttons per feature card, the /api/action allowlist,
CLI commands are unconditional — the state machine does not block CLI usage.
isActionValid() is imported but not yet called in the CLI command handlers.
Action modes: terminal → open native tmux session
fire-and-forget → sync CLI call · agent → run agent session
(feature-70 replaces WebSocket with native terminal for 'terminal' mode)
getSessionAction() decides: create-and-start vs attach vs send-keys
based on current tmux session state.
Drive = single agent on branch · Fleet = multiple agents in worktrees
